Amendments2022—Subsec. (a)(4). , amended par. (4) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (4) read as follows: “Authorize personnel designated by the Director to carry firearms to the extent necessary for the performance of the Agency’s authorized functions, except that, within the United States, such authority shall be limited to the purposes of protection of classified materials and information, the training of Agency personnel and other authorized persons in the use of firearms, the protection of Agency and Office of the Director of National Intelligence installations and property, the protection of current and former Agency personnel and their immediate families, defectors and their immediate families, and other persons in the United States under Agency auspices, and the protection of the Director of National Intelligence and current and former personnel of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence and their immediate families as the Director of National Intelligence may designate;”.Pub. L. 117–263, § 6414(a), inserted “and Office of the Director of National Intelligence” after “protection of Agency”.Pub. L. 117–263, § 6303(b)Subsec. (a)(7), (8). , realigned margins.Pub. L. 117–263, § 6414(c)2019—Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “,” for “.,”.Pub. L. 116–92, § 6411(1)section 3036 of this titlesection 3036 of this titleSubsec. (a)(4). , substituted “current and former personnel of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence and their immediate families as the Director of National Intelligence may designate;” for “such personnel of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence as the Director of National Intelligence may designate;”.Pub. L. 116–92, § 6401Subsec. (a)(8). –(4), added par. (8).Pub. L. 116–92, § 6411(2)2010—Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “authorized under .” for “authorized under paragraphs (2) and (3) of , subsections (c)(7) and (d) of , subsections (a) and (g) of , and ”.Pub. (a)(4). , substituted “the protection of current” for “and the protection of current” and inserted “, and the protection of the Director of National Intelligence and such personnel of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence as the Director of National Intelligence may designate” before the semicolon.Pub. L. 111–259, § 4212003—Subsec. (a)(1). substituted “(c)(7)” for “(c)(6)”.Pub. L. 108–1772002—Subsec. (c)(2). substituted “a report on the transfer of sums described in paragraph (1) each time that authority is exercised.” for “an annual report on the transfers of sums described in paragraph (1).”Pub. L. 107–3062000— added subsec. (a) and (b) headings and subsec. (c).Pub. L. 106–5671998—Subsec. (7) and subsec. (b).Pub. L. 105–107June 30, 1932Provided1993—Subsec. (a). substituted “Office of Management and Budget” for “Bureau of the Budget” and “subparagraphs (B) and (C) of , subsections (c)(5) and (d) of , subsections (a) and (g) of , and ” for “sections 403 and 405 of this title”.Pub. Historical Advisory Panel of Central Intelligence Agency, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–263, div. F, title LXIV, § 6413136 Stat. 3527Dec. 23, 2022“(a).—Sense of CongressIt is the sense of Congress that Congress expresses its appreciation—“(1)to the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency for reconstituting the Historical Advisory Panel; and“(2)for the important work of the Historical Advisory Panel, especially for—“(A)the efforts of the Panel to aid with the declassification of materials that enrich the historical national security record; and“(B)the assistance of the Panel in liaison with the scholarly community.“(b).—Reporting RequirementThe Historical Advisory Panel shall report directly to the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency.“(c).—Historical Advisory Panel DefinedThe term ‘Historical Advisory Panel’ means the panel of the Central Intelligence Agency, regardless of the name of the panel, that assists in conducting declassification reviews and providing other assistance with respect to matters of historical interest.”

children.text: Transfer to and receive from other Government agencies such sums as may be approved by the Office of Management and Budget, for the performance of any of the functions or activities authorized under , and any other Government agency is authorized to transfer to or receive from the Agency such sums without regard to any provisions of law limiting or prohibiting transfers between appropriations. Sums transferred to the Agency in accordance with this paragraph may be expended for the purposes and under the authority of this chapter without regard to limitations of appropriations from which transferred;section 3036 of this title
Exchange funds without regard to section 3651 of the Revised Statutes;

Reimburse other Government agencies for services of personnel assigned to the Agency, and such other Government agencies are authorized, without regard to provisions of law to the contrary, so to assign or detail any officer or employee for duty with the Agency;

children.label: A
children.text: the training of Agency personnel and other authorized persons in the use of firearms;

children.label: B
children.text: the protection of classified materials and information;

children.label: C
children.text: the protection of installations and property of the Agency;

children.label: i
children.text: current and former Agency personnel and their immediate families;

children.label: ii
children.text: individuals nominated by the President to the position of Director (including with respect to an individual whom a President-elect (as defined in section 3(c) of the Presidential Transition Act of 1963 ( note) has declared an intent to nominate) and their immediate families; and3 U.S.C. 102

children.label: iii
children.text: defectors and their immediate families, and other persons in the United States under Agency auspices; and

children.label: i
children.text: installations and property of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Director of National Intelligence and the immediate family of the Director;

children.label: iii
children.text: current and former personnel of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence and their immediate families as the Director of National Intelligence may designate; and

children.label: iv
children.text: individuals nominated by the President to the position of Director of National Intelligence (including with respect to an individual whom a President-elect has declared an intent to nominate) and their immediate families;
Make alterations, improvements, and repairs on premises rented by the Agency, and pay rent therefor;

Determine and fix the minimum and maximum limits of age within which an original appointment may be made to an operational position within the Agency, notwithstanding the provision of any other law, in accordance with such criteria as the Director, in his discretion, may prescribe;
Notwithstanding , enter into multiyear leases for up to 15 years; andsection 1341(a)(1) of title 31

Upon the approval of the Director, provide, during any fiscal year, with or without reimbursement, subsistence to any personnel assigned to an overseas location designated by the Agency as an austere location.

children.heading: Scope of authority for expenditure
children.label: b Scope of authority for expenditure
A
children.text: the entire lease; or

children.label: B
children.text: the first 12 months of the lease and the Government’s estimated termination liability.

children.label: A
children.text: such lease shall include a clause that provides that the contract shall be terminated if budget authority (as defined by ) is not provided specifically for that project in an appropriations Act in advance of an obligation of funds in respect thereto;section 622(2) of title 2

children.label: B
children.text: notwithstanding , amounts obligated for paying termination costs with respect to such lease shall remain available until the costs associated with termination of such lease are paid;section 1552 of title 31

children.label: C
children.text: funds available for termination liability shall remain available to satisfy rental obligations with respect to such lease in subsequent fiscal years in the event such lease is not terminated early, but only to the extent those funds are in excess of the amount of termination liability at the time of their use to satisfy such rental obligations; and

children.label: D
children.text: funds appropriated for a fiscal year may be used to make payments on such lease, for a maximum of 12 months, beginning any time during such fiscal year.

children.heading: Transfers for acquisition of land
children.label: c Transfers for acquisition of land

children.text: Sums appropriated or otherwise made available to the Agency for the acquisition of land that are transferred to another department or agency for that purpose shall remain available for 3 years.

The Director shall submit to the Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate and the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives a report on the transfer of sums described in paragraph (1) each time that authority is exercised.
